subject: The 21-day threshold
hook: People say it takes 21 days to make a habit. The real story is less tidy and more interesting, and you're standing in the middle of it.

subject: Thirty days present
hook: Thirty days ago you put the ring on. Before that, you answered a handful of quiet questions — remember the quiz? You answered them as someone slightly different. Someone who was somewhere else a little more often.

subject: What autopilot costs
hook: Some good news first, because it's true: attention comes back. It isn't gone. It's just spoken for.

subject: Before it arrives
hook: Your ring is a few days out. Between now and then there are exactly two things worth doing, and together they take less than five minutes.

subject: Charge, pair, breathe
hook: The box is open, or about to be. Three small steps stand between you and your first pulse — and only one of them is for tonight.

subject: Come back to now
hook: There's a dish, or a drawer, or a windowsill. The ring is probably there now.

subject: Confirm your size
hook: The sizer has done its work. Tap the size that fit this morning — the one that cleared the knuckle and sat still — and your ring ships within two days.

subject: Day one: just wear it
hook: The ring is on your finger. That's the whole job today.

subject: Exchange, made simple
hook: Your ring arrived and the size isn't right. That happens — fingers are not standard parts. The exchange is free, and it is short.

subject: The First Hundred
hook: There are about a hundred people who shaped what Pulse is becoming. Not investors. Not advisors. People who wore the ring through real mornings, told the truth about it, and let us pass that truth on.

subject: Be Here WOW
hook: Thirty days ago the ring went on your finger. Today I want to ask you for something small.

subject: A gift of presence
hook: Your gift is confirmed. Somewhere, someone is about to get their attention back — and it comes from you.

subject: Give a month of presence
hook: A month ago, you'd have wanted someone to point. That's all this is.

subject: How is it going with your ring?
hook: Four days of wearing. I have exactly one question, and it takes one tap.

subject: It's here
hook: Your ring is at your door. One ask, and then I'll leave you to it.

subject: Your launch kit
hook: The pilot is agreed and the rings are on their way. This kit is everything launch day needs, in three pieces.

subject: No rush
hook: Your ring arrived a few days ago. I'm not writing to hurry you.

subject: It's on its way
hook: This is the email you've been waiting for. Your ring left us this morning.

subject: One minute, no ring required
hook: This one asks for nothing. Not the ring, not the app, not even a quiet room.

subject: Your ring is coming
hook: Order received. Everything below is confirmed.

subject: The pause, by text
hook: Most Wednesdays, somewhere around midday, we send one line by text. Not a picture. Not an offer. One sentence, then nothing.

subject: The pause is working
hook: Ten days in, this is usually where a company would show you a chart. Your numbers, your week in review. We don't have one. The ring doesn't count anything, so neither can I.

subject: Join the Presence Circle
hook: Six weeks in. This invitation waits for that, on purpose — it means something different now than it would have on day two.

subject: The Presence Experience
hook: One thing to hand you today, and it isn't a pitch.

subject: A pulse instead of a ping
hook: The last two notes were about attention leaving. Today, the part I started this company to say: what gently brings it back.

subject: Your questions, answered
hook: By now you know what the ring does. A few times an hour, a small pulse on your finger, and you come back to the room you're in. What's usually left are the practical questions. Here they are, answered plainly.

subject: Your presence score
hook: You answered honestly. Most people don't.

subject: One reader's Tuesday, after
hook: A few notes ago I told you about a Tuesday: 6:50am, the toast, the nod on a half-second delay. This week a reader named Johanna wrote to me about her Tuesday. Her quiz showed the same pattern as yours — {{archetype}}. I

subject: First, the right fit
hook: Before order {{order_number}} ships, one question. Do you know your ring size?

subject: Help shape what's next
hook: We're building next year's Pulse now. I'd rather build it with you than near you.

subject: Here's what you can share
hook: By now someone has probably asked you about the ring. At the sink, in the car park, over someone else's shoulder at work. That question can be strangely hard to answer.

subject: You're showing others the way
hook: Your story went where you said it could — {{shared_where}}. I want to show you one thing it did there.

subject: Your sizing kit is here
hook: Your sizing kit was delivered today. No rush — good fit is a one-evening job, and it's worth doing slowly.

subject: Someone thought of you
hook: There's a box on its way to you, or maybe already in your hands. {{gift_sender}} sent it. Inside is a gold ring that hums gently a few times an hour — not to alert you to anything, but to bring you back to where you alre

subject: Someone you love is on autopilot too
hook: Near the end of the quiz, we asked who you most want to be present for.

subject: Your space assessment
hook: Next comes the assessment. Eight minutes, once — the only homework in this whole process.

subject: We received your inquiry
hook: Your note arrived. Thank you for thinking about your people this way.

subject: Your proposal
hook: Your proposal is ready — it's attached. This note is just the cover.

subject: Questions answered
hook: You've had the proposal a few days, so you have questions. Here are the four we hear most, answered plainly.

subject: Still thinking it over
cta: Pick up where you left off
hook: You started an order the other day and stopped partway. No guilt in that. Pausing before you spend money on your own attention is exactly the instinct the ring is built around.

subject: Your story kit
hook: Two months in, you tell this better than we ever could. So this isn't a script — it's a kit. Tell it your way, wherever you already talk to people.

subject: The Sunday Pause — Arrive
hook: It's Sunday evening. Somewhere in the house, tomorrow is already clearing its throat.

subject: Month one: your team's presence report
hook: One month in. Here is your team's presence report — their own words, read as a group.

subject: Can we tell your story?
hook: I have an ask, and I want to make it easy to refuse.

subject: The moment you noticed
hook: It's 6:50 on a Tuesday morning. Maya is in her kitchen, buttering toast she won't remember buttering. The radio is on. She couldn't tell you what it's saying.

subject: Try this before you buy anything
hook: Before anything else, I want to give you the thing itself. You don't need to buy anything for this to work. Not the ring, not an app, not a cushion. Just the next sixty seconds.

subject: Two weeks of returning
hook: Two weeks ago the ring was new on your finger. Now it's just yours. I want to name what happened in between, because nobody else will: you returned. Not once. Many times, in small ways nobody saw.

subject: We noticed your practice
hook: This isn't a campaign. I'm writing because you wrote first.

subject: Week one: what to notice
hook: One week with the ring. Here is the thing nobody tells you about a practice this small: it works before you notice it working.

subject: We'll be here
hook: A short note about these letters themselves.

subject: What did you notice?
hook: Three days of small taps. Today, one small question.

subject: What others noticed this week
hook: The week is nearly done. Sunday's theme was Arrive — arriving in the room you're already in. Here is what three people in the Presence Circle noticed. Shared with permission, first names only.

subject: What you've given yourself
hook: No ask today. Just a small mirror.

subject: When would now be a good time?
hook: There's no countdown in this letter. Nothing expires at midnight. Just where things stand, so the decision can be quiet.

subject: Your story matters
hook: Last week I asked for an impression. This is a different ask, and a bigger one.
